answer:
So in my last role as at GO-OUT i had customers from all over the world- Israel, Greece, Canada, US, South America, UK, Thailand, India and so on. From the US we had costumers from NY, Miami and LA from the nightlife industry and concerts. 
Some of my time at GO-OUT i had an employee under me that he lived in NY and i manage him and he was with those clients and after a while we dicided to fire him and me and my other team deal with those clients. We created a seats map for the concerts for each event hall, at the day of the event stayed up all night helping our costumers remotly and some of the time sent someone to be our voice and we had to deal with everything from here. There was a cases when our system did not work well with the seats map so we work very hard to make our customers happy. Beside that, The nightlife industry in Israel is very hard, our customers allways need help with things that does not necessarily connected to our system and so the job was to help them when ever they need - 24/7 - even in hollydays, Fridays, Saturdays and so on.

Of course! At GO-OUT I did a lot of renewal process, upsell conversations and expansion of partner contracts. Part of my job from the beggining was to bring more clients. we did not had at that moment a sellsperson so the Customer Success team had to do that. We sign with new and existing customer in new contarcts, found new customers from the indusrty, brought them to a meeting and closed them. Every day I had at least 2 of those things. I closed more the new 200 customers while working at GO-OUT.

One of the main problems we had was that the payment method took a lot of time and as a result we lost a lot of customers at the payment stage. It used to be very clear that you had to enter the credit card details and carry out a full payment process. Today, in the era of automation and where everything is easy to do, you can't be left behind, especially when it comes to the final process of making the payment. Many customers contacted me and shared that they were losing end customers at the payment stage and that they didn't understand why they didn't have Google Wallet or Apple Wallet and secondly to make the payment. For a period of time, the company and the product preferred to emphasize other improvements, but because I knew the needs of the customers and their customers, I pushed for it to be implemented and indeed after about a month of testing we saw that 40% of the payments were made using this feature and after two months more than 60% were using it, and so I understood that sometimes the product and development team have their own desires and their own roadmap for how to develop, but the people on the ground know best in some cases what is right and what is wrong.
At GO-OUT we could not track any KPIs. Nothing. The management of the company did nto do anything to track KPIs or our work so i do not have anything on that.

Yeah with my worldwide customers i had to speak read and write in english. Everyday i spoke or red or wrote with them - they were not my major clients and i did not do that all of the day but yeah at least every day i did one of those and i was the manager of the CS i had in Greece and i talked a lot with our sellsperson in Greece so i spoke a lot in english while working there.
